# Yu Feng

> Associate Professor, Computer Science, UC Santa Barbara

Yu Feng is an associate professor in computer science at UC Santa Barbara. His research focuses on programming languages, formal verification, security, program synthesis, and AI agent safety.

## Research Areas
- Formal methods and security for AI agents
- Zero-knowledge proof compilers and verifiers
- Smart contract and DeFi security
- Program synthesis for end-user programming

## Links
- Homepage: https://fredfeng.github.io/
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=urQ9fNgAAAAJ
- GitHub: https://github.com/fredfeng
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/captain8299
- Email: yufeng@cs.ucsb.edu

## Selected Publications
- Tabby: A Synthesis-Aided Compiler for High-Performance Zero-Knowledge Proof Circuits, OOPSLA 2025
- Automated Repair of OpenID Connect Programs, ASE 2025
- Tessel: An Optimizing Compiler for Efficient Zero-Knowledge Circuits, SBC 2025
- FORAY: Towards Effective Attack Synthesis against Deep Logical Vulnerabilities in DeFi Protocols, CCS 2024
- AuthSaber: Automated Safety Verification of OpenID Connect Programs, CCS 2024
- Refinement Types for Visualization, ASE 2024
- Certifying Zero-Knowledge Circuits with Refinement Types, IEEE Security & Privacy (Oakland) 2024
- Practical Security Analysis of Zero-Knowledge Proof Circuits, USENIX Security 2024
- Fast and Reliable Program Synthesis via User Interaction, ASE 2023
- Conflict-Driven Synthesis for Layout Engines, PLDI 2023

## Education
- Ph.D. in Computer Science, University of Texas at Austin, 2018
- M.S. in Computer Science, Beihang University, 2008
